acceptance_criteria.text: The current failure mode is explained from repository code: batch folder input scans only direct children and therefore misses videos under `input/videos` when the selected folder is `input`.

proof.summary: Documents the pre-patch direct-child `iterdir()` scan and why nested `input/videos` files were missed.

acceptance_criteria.text: `run_surface_decoder_colab.ipynb` is updated so batch folder input discovers `.mp4` and `.avi` videos recursively under the selected folder.

proof.command: rg -n 'DEFAULT_INPUT_PATH|requested_path\.rglob|requested_path\.iterdir|_mp4_keys|No \.mp4/\.avi files found|Videos found|recursively for sequential' run_surface_decoder_colab.ipynb
proof.summary: Static search confirms `requested_path.rglob("*")` is present and the direct-only batch scan is absent from the relevant output.

acceptance_criteria.text: The existing AVI/MP4 duplicate handling remains in place and works for nested files by comparing stems within the same parent folder.

proof.command: python -c "from pathlib import Path; import tempfile; ..."
proof.summary: Temporary nested folder test retains `other/a.avi`, skips duplicate `videos/a.avi`, and keeps `videos/a.mp4` plus `videos/b.avi`.

acceptance_criteria.text: User-visible batch messaging no longer says only "MP4 videos found" when AVI files are accepted, and the no-files error mentions recursive search.

proof.summary: Static search confirms recursive no-files message, generic `Videos found`, and recursive batch log text.

acceptance_criteria.text: The notebook remains valid JSON and the proof-loop artifacts validate with an overall `PASS`.

proof.command: python -m json.tool run_surface_decoder_colab.ipynb > $null; if ($LASTEXITCODE -eq 0) { 'notebook json ok' }

commands_for_fresh_verifier: python -m json.tool run_surface_decoder_colab.ipynb > $null; if ($LASTEXITCODE -eq 0) { 'notebook json ok' }
rg -n 'DEFAULT_INPUT_PATH|requested_path\.rglob|requested_path\.iterdir|_mp4_keys|No \.mp4/\.avi files found|Videos found|recursively for sequential' run_surface_decoder_colab.ipynb

python -c "from pathlib import Path; import tempfile; exts={'.mp4','.avi'}; d=Path(tempfile.mkdtemp()); (d/'videos').mkdir(); (d/'other').mkdir(); (d/'videos'/'a.avi').write_text('', encoding='utf-8'); (d/'videos'/'a.mp4').write_text('', encoding='utf-8'); (d/'other'/'a.avi').write_text('', encoding='utf-8'); (d/'videos'/'b.avi').write_text('', encoding='utf-8'); files=sorted(p for p in d.rglob('*') if p.is_file() and p.suffix.lower() in exts); keys={(p.parent,p.stem) for p in files if p.suffix.lower()=='.mp4'}; files=[p for p in files if not (p.suffix.lower()=='.avi' and (p.parent,p.stem) in keys)]; result=[p.relative_to(d).as_posix() for p in files]; print(result); assert result == ['other/a.avi', 'videos/a.mp4', 'videos/b.avi']"
